prompt:
Take the provided reference image and reproduce it PIXEL-FAITHFULLY — same diamond-gem silhouette pack, same two-hand composition gripping the left and right widest corners, same thumb pressing through the pack onto the activation oval, same line-art style on pure white background, same line weight. DO NOT change the pack shape, the hand poses, the line weight, or the style.

TWO MODIFICATIONS:

1) MOVE THE LOGO DOWN TO THE VERTICAL MIDDLE — CRITICAL: in the reference the DIALED HEALTH logo is at the TOP of the pack. This is WRONG. The logo must be at the VERTICAL CENTER of the diamond-gem pack — at the same vertical height as the GRIPPING HANDS (between the LEFT widest corner and the RIGHT widest corner). The logo's vertical center should line up with the pack's widest horizontal point (the middle of the gem). DELETE the logo from the top area entirely; draw a NEW logo at the dead-middle vertical position of the pack. The upper area (just below the flat top edge) is now EMPTY white space — no logo there at all. The logo is centered horizontally AND vertically on the pack.

2) SHRINK THE OVAL OBJECT: make the activation oval inside the pack SMALLER — roughly HALF its current size. The oval is now a small horizontal ellipse, much more compact, easier for a single thumb to press. Position it BELOW the now-centered logo (in the lower half of the pack, near the bottom point) where the thumb presses on it from outside the pack.

Key requirement: the LOGO is in the MIDDLE of the pack vertically, NOT at the top. Anywhere near the top edge is wrong.

Do not modify anything else — same diamond-gem shape, same hands, same thumb-on-oval pose, same line weight, same pure black line-art on white. No frame, no border, no other labels.
